validator_description: Multiple small pleural calcifications both sides in lower parts, unchanged
management_impact: No management change — calcifications noted as unchanged, incidental finding
notes: Doctor did not mention pleural calcifications; validator notes them as unchanged from prior. No clinical management impact.

validator_description: Multiple fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles — may correspond to consequence of ischemic heart disease
management_impact: No immediate management change; noted as possible IHD sequela, no new workup triggered beyond existing cardiac evaluation
notes: Doctor omitted myocardial fatty inclusions. Validator adds clinical context (possible IHD sequela) but does not recommend additional workup beyond existing PCP referral.

validator_description: Pericardial effusion thickness 6 mm (previously in smaller volume)
management_impact: No immediate management change — 6 mm is small; however validator notes it is increasing from prior, which may warrant monitoring
notes: Doctor called it 'no significant pericardial effusion'; validator quantifies it at 6 mm and notes it has increased compared to prior study. This is a clinically notable characterization difference but does not trigger immediate intervention.

validator_description: Mild aortic and mitral valve calcifications
management_impact: No management change — mild mitral valve calcification is incidental at this age
notes: Doctor reported aortic valve calcification but omitted mitral valve calcification documented by validator.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary calcification (more pronounced in LAD and proximal RCA). PCP consultation recommended.
validator_description: CAC in left main, LAD, LCx, RCA — qualitatively moderate. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: No management change — both recommend PCP consultation; doctor did not enumerate left main and LCx involvement
notes: Doctor identified LAD and RCA but omitted left main and LCx. Validator documents all four vessels. Both grade as moderate and recommend PCP. Incomplete vessel enumeration under ACRad44.

doctor_description: Main pulmonary artery dilated up to 4.0 cm
validator_description: Main pulmonary artery dilated up to 3.7 cm, unchanged
management_impact: No management change — both note dilation suggesting possible pulmonary hypertension; measurement discrepancy only
notes: Doctor measures 4.0 cm vs validator 3.7 cm. Both interpret as possible pulmonary hypertension. Measurement difference does not change management.

validator_description: Multiple small hypodense liver lesions up to 5 mm, unchanged. For low-risk patients: no follow-up needed. For high-risk patients: follow-up MRI 3–6 months.
management_impact: Potential management impact for high-risk patients (MRI follow-up); for low-risk no change. Doctor omission means risk stratification not communicated.
notes: Doctor omitted multiple small hypodense liver lesions. Validator notes them as unchanged and provides risk-stratified follow-up guidance per Measure 405 context. Does not fully qualify as Measure 405 (which targets benign-confirmed lesions with explicit no-follow-up statement).

doctor_description: Moderate coronary calcification. CAC: Agatston score 51, CAC-DRS A1.
validator_description: CAC in left main, LAD, RCA — qualitatively moderate. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: No management change — both identify moderate CAC and recommend PCP; doctor provides Agatston score but does not name specific vessels; validator names vessels but lacks Agatston score
notes: Doctor provides quantitative Agatston score (51) and CAC-DRS classification but omits vessel-level detail. Validator names affected vessels (left main, LAD, RCA) without quantitative score. Both recommend PCP. Incomplete per ACRad44 vessel enumeration.

validator_description: Mild enlargement of left ventricle
management_impact: No immediate management change — mild LV enlargement is incidental finding; already captured under cardiac evaluation context
notes: Doctor noted no cardiomegaly overall but did not identify mild LV enlargement specifically called out by validator.

validator_description: Mild bronchial wall thickening
management_impact: No management change — mild bronchial wall thickening is non-specific and does not trigger additional workup in this context
notes: Doctor did not report bronchial wall thickening noted by validator. Clinically non-specific finding.

doctor_description: Diffusely heterogeneous thyroid with calcific inclusions. Thyroid ultrasound recommended (per ACR 2014).
validator_description: Heterogeneous thyroid gland, consistent with ultrasound data from October 14, 2025, requires no follow-up.
management_impact: Doctor recommends thyroid ultrasound; validator states no follow-up needed given concordance with known prior ultrasound (October 2025). Management differs — doctor's recommendation for ultrasound is potentially unnecessary given existing recent ultrasound data.
notes: Doctor recommends thyroid ultrasound unaware of (or without referencing) the existing October 2025 ultrasound. Validator contextualizes with prior study and correctly states no follow-up needed. Doctor's recommendation is an overrecommendation in this context but based on imaging appearance alone is not unreasonable.

anatomical_location: Right upper lobe, right middle lobe, left upper lobe
validator_description: 3 mm nodule RUL (image 60), 2 mm nodule RML (image 95), 3 mm nodule LUL (image 72) — Fleischner: low-risk no follow-up; high-risk generally no follow-up (optional CT at 12 months if high suspicion)
management_impact: No follow-up required per Fleischner 2017 for nodules ≤4 mm in both low- and high-risk patients — management equivalent. However doctor did not document the nodules at all, missing the risk-stratified Fleischner statement.
notes: Doctor stated 'no nodules requiring follow-up' without identifying the small nodules. Validator identifies three solid nodules up to 3 mm and correctly applies Fleischner. For a 35-year-old female, these are genuinely low-risk findings with no required follow-up. Omission of the nodules themselves (not just follow-up) is the discrepancy. Grade 2b — no management change but documentation incomplete per Fleischner 2017 standard.

validator_description: Few areas of air trapping, clinically insignificant
management_impact: No management change — validator explicitly notes clinically insignificant
notes: Doctor did not document air trapping noted by validator. Noted as clinically insignificant.

validator_description: Residual thymus tissue
management_impact: No management change — residual thymic tissue in a 35-year-old is a normal variant
notes: Doctor omitted residual thymic tissue. Normal variant in a young patient; no clinical consequence.

validator_description: Ossifications along periphery of humeral heads (consistent with shoulders radiograph 2/20/2026)
management_impact: No management change — consistent with prior imaging, incidental degenerative finding
notes: Doctor did not document peripheral humeral head ossifications. Validator contextualizes with prior shoulder radiograph. No clinical consequence.

doctor_description: CAC qualitatively mild. PCP consultation recommended.
validator_description: CAC in left main, LAD, LCx — qualitatively moderate. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: No management change — both recommend PCP consultation. Severity grading differs (mild vs moderate) and vessel enumeration differs.
notes: Doctor grades CAC as mild without naming vessels; validator grades as moderate and identifies left main, LAD, LCx. Severity discrepancy (mild vs moderate) is clinically notable but both reach same management endpoint (PCP referral). Incomplete per ACRad44 — doctor omits vessel-level detail and uses lower severity grade.

validator_description: Aortic and mitral valve calcifications
management_impact: No management change — mild mitral valve calcification is incidental
notes: Doctor documented aortic valve calcification but omitted mitral valve calcification.

doctor_description: Decreased height L1 vertebral body by 25%
validator_description: Hemangioma L1 up to 2.0 cm, no follow-up required
management_impact: No management change — validator specifies hemangioma with no follow-up; doctor notes height loss but does not characterize the lesion type
notes: Doctor reports decreased height of L1 (25%) but does not identify or characterize the hemangioma. Validator identifies a 2.0 cm hemangioma at L1 and explicitly states no follow-up. These may represent overlapping findings at the same level. Characterization discrepancy.

anatomical_location: Common hepatic duct and common bile duct
validator_description: Common hepatic and common bile ducts dilated up to 1.0 cm. Gallbladder contracted. No calculi in visualized ductal lumina. Consider abdominal ultrasound.
management_impact: Biliary ductal dilation up to 1.0 cm in an 84-year-old female warrants further evaluation — validator recommends abdominal ultrasound to exclude obstructive pathology (e.g., choledocholithiasis, stricture, or malignancy). Doctor completely omitted this finding.
notes: Doctor missed common hepatic and CBD dilation to 1.0 cm in an 84-year-old. This measurement is at the upper limit of normal for the elderly and the validator specifically recommends abdominal ultrasound. Omission would delay evaluation of potential biliary obstruction — management-changing finding. Graded 3.

validator_description: Thyroid right lobe hypodense nodule up to 4 mm — no follow-up
management_impact: No management change — validator explicitly states no follow-up for this sub-centimeter nodule in an 84-year-old
notes: Doctor omitted 4 mm thyroid nodule. Validator documents it and correctly applies no-follow-up guidance. For an 84-year-old patient, this is appropriate. Consistent with Measure 406 (inverse) — correct behavior is not to recommend follow-up for nodules <1 cm.

doctor_description: Mild ground-glass opacities both lungs, interlobular septal thickening with mosaic attenuation pattern. Bronchial walls irregularly thickened.
validator_description: Diffuse mosaic attenuation pattern both lungs, interlobular septal thickening in lung apices. No signs of interstitial disease (honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, reticulations). Mild bronchial wall thickening. May be consistent with obliterative small airways disease or vascular disease including pulmonary hypertension.
management_impact: No management change — both note the pattern; validator adds differential (small airways vs pulmonary hypertension) and explicitly excludes interstitial disease. Doctor describes GGO and irregular bronchial thickening without differential context.
notes: Doctor describes GGO and irregular bronchial wall thickening. Validator reframes as mosaic attenuation without GGO and provides differential diagnosis. The characterization differs but neither recommends specific additional workup beyond existing follow-up. Clinically the interpretations overlap.

anatomical_location: Right lower lobe subpleural, left lower lobe subpleural
validator_description: Right lower lobe subpleural nodule 5 mm (image 179). Left lower lobe subpleural nodule 3 mm (image 196). Fleischner: low-risk no follow-up; high-risk generally no follow-up (optional CT at 12 months).
management_impact: No follow-up required per Fleischner for these sizes in most patients; however in a high-risk 73-year-old with severe emphysema, optional CT at 12 months may be considered. Doctor omission means risk-stratified Fleischner statement was not provided.
notes: Doctor stated no nodules requiring follow-up without identifying the subpleural nodules. Validator identifies 5 mm RLL and 3 mm LLL subpleural nodules. In the context of severe emphysema (independent lung cancer risk factor), these are clinically relevant to document even if no mandatory follow-up is required. Grade 2b — documentation failure per Fleischner 2017.

doctor_description: Multiple areas of mixed emphysema, total involvement 17%. Emphysema = independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider LDCT if eligible.
validator_description: Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. Emphysema = independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider LDCT if eligible.
management_impact: No management change — both identify emphysema as lung cancer risk factor and recommend LDCT consideration. Type characterization differs (mixed 17% vs severe confluent centrilobular).
notes: Doctor characterizes as mixed emphysema with quantitative involvement (17%); validator characterizes as severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. Both reach same management recommendation (LDCT). Qualitative characterization difference only. Both fulfill QMM23 requirements.

doctor_description: Calcifications in walls of coronary arteries: Agatston score 47, CAC-DRS A1. PCP consultation recommended.
validator_description: CAC in left main, LAD, LCx, RCA — qualitatively moderate. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: No management change — both recommend PCP consultation. Doctor provides Agatston score but calls it 'mild' (A1) vs validator's 'moderate' with four-vessel enumeration.
notes: Doctor provides quantitative score (Agatston 47, CAC-DRS A1) but does not enumerate affected vessels and does not name four vessels with left main involvement. Validator identifies left main, LAD, LCx, RCA as moderate. Discrepancy in severity grading and vessel-level documentation. Both recommend PCP. Incomplete per ACRad44.

doctor_description: Few calcifications left upper lobe 6 mm and 4 mm — postinflammatory. No follow-up.
validator_description: Calcified nodules left upper lobe 4 mm and 3 mm (series 5, images 47–52) — healed granulomas, no follow-up.
management_impact: No management change — both state no follow-up. Measurement discrepancy: doctor 6 mm and 4 mm vs validator 4 mm and 3 mm.
notes: Doctor measures the larger calcified nodule at 6 mm; validator at 4 mm. Both correctly identify as postinflammatory/granuloma with no follow-up required. Measurement discrepancy only.

validator_description: Moderate degenerative changes spine.
management_impact: No management change — severity characterization differs (severe vs moderate) but neither triggers specific additional management
notes: Doctor grades degenerative changes as severe (C6–L2); validator grades as moderate. No management implications for either grade in this context.
